channel.title: GitHub Issues on Rob Sewell (aka SQL DBA With A Beard)
channel.description: Recent content in GitHub Issues on Rob Sewell (aka SQL DBA With A Beard)

item.title: GitHub Action Workflow Protected branch update failed
item.description:
The last post showed how we created an easy process to update a web-page using a GitHub Issue and two GitHub Actions.

Protecting the repository

I opened the repository in the browser and GitHub and was provided with a warning that said

Clicking on the protect this branch button gave the reasoning.

Protect your most important branches Branch protection rules define whether collaborators can delete or force push to the branch and set requirements for any pushes to the branch, such as passing status checks or a linear commit history.

item.title: Creating A Training Day Speakers List with GitHub Action from a GitHub Issue
item.description:
The last post showed the resource that we created to enable speakers to let events know that they have content for pre-cons/training days. This post will describe how the automation was created using a GitHub Issue and two GitHub Actions.

What do we need?

The idea was to have a form for user input that could easily allow a person to add themselves and some information to a web page. The page holds a list of speakers who can present training day sessions for data platform events. The web page can be found here. This page is generated from a JSON file.
